2605.19848.title: CLIF: Concept-Level Influence Functions for Transparent Bottleneck Models
2605.19848.paper_summary: In recent years, the black-box nature of deep learning models has limited their application in high-stakes domains such as medical diagnosis and finance, where interpretability is essential. To address this, we propose a novel approach using influence functions to enhance interpretability in NLP models at both the sample and concept levels. Experiments on CEBaB and Yelp datasets show that influence functions effectively identify the most impactful training samples, both helpful and harmful, on model predictions. By adjusting the labels and weights of these samples, we demonstrate that model performance can be restored to baseline levels without retraining, confirming the value of influence functions for efficient data debugging. Furthermore, our concept-level analysis identifies key concepts within Concept Bottleneck Models (CBM) that significantly affect predictions. Modifying these concepts alters model behavior observably, providing clear insights into the decision process.
2605.19848.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，深度學習模型的黑箱特性限制了它們在醫療診斷和金融等高風險領域的應用，這些領域對可解釋性有著重要需求。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新穎的方法，使用影響函數來增強自然語言處理模型在樣本和概念層面的可解釋性。對CEBaB和Yelp數據集的實驗顯示，影響函數有效地識別出對模型預測影響最大的訓練樣本，包括有幫助和有害的樣本。通過調整這些樣本的標籤和權重，我們證明模型性能可以在不重新訓練的情況下恢復到基線水平，確認了影響函數在高效數據調試中的價值。此外，我們的概念層級分析識別了在概念瓶頸模型（CBM）中顯著影響預測的關鍵概念。修改這些概念會明顯改變模型行為，提供對決策過程的清晰洞察。
2605.19848.author: Yike Sun et.al.
2605.19848.authors: Yike Sun, Mingkun Xu, Mu You, Zhongzhi He, Henghua Shen, Zehan Tan, Derek F. Wong, Tao Fang
